ksa_text: Knowledge of comprehensive AI evaluation frameworks, including functional performance testing, behavioral testing, red-teaming, fairness evaluation, robustness testing, and safety evaluation.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of tools for testing the robustness and resilience of AI products and solutions.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of performance tuning tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA RM-K-006 (Risk Management/Knowledge) scores 0.514 composite (highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.514). Scope is edge_case for Clinical AI Specialist: 0 in-scope signals, 0 out-of-scope signals. Seniority is appropriate.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with edge_case scope, Risk Management, composite 0.514. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support Risk Management for Clinical AI Specialist?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Risk Management appears in ~40% of Clinical AI Specialist postings. Clinical risk management overlaps with AI safety and post-market surveillance. Zero role-specific signals insufficient for edge inclusion at 40% market penetration.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. Risk Management without role signals not market-supported. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. Risk Management without role signals not market-supported. Final decision: exclude.
